items.text: We continue to evaluate low carbon opportunities for potential future investment in support of our operational emission reduction targets.
items.rationale: Generic statement about evaluating low carbon opportunities; not a risk disclosure.

items.text: Additionally, legislation has been introduced in certain U.S. states that would provide Attorneys General, insurers and individuals a right to recover against certain energy companies for alleged climate change impacts.
items.rationale: Introduces specific new legislative risk allowing legal claims against energy companies for climate impacts.

items.text: Should such legislation become law, we may be exposed to additional, significant liabilities.
items.rationale: Directly quantifies the liability exposure introduced by the new legislation risk, part of the same newly introduced risk.

items.text: However, in 2025 the EPA moved to dismantle some climate-related regulations (e.g. delaying compliance deadlines for methane standards and proposing to eliminate most obligations under the Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program).
items.rationale: Describes a specific new regulatory development (EPA rollback of methane/reporting rules) creating new compliance uncertainty.

items.text: These policy swings create additional uncertainty for companies who need to plan for operations that will endure through administrations.
items.rationale: General framing statement about uncertainty from policy swings, not a specific new risk.

items.text: These regulatory changes may also complicate our ability to access non-operated or joint venture emissions data to complete our inventory of emissions.
items.rationale: Elaborates on how regulatory changes could impair emissions data access, expanding on the previously introduced regulatory risk.

items.text: Additionally, international climate initiatives, such as the United Nations Conference of the Parties summits, continue to shape the global response to climate change.
items.rationale: General background statement about international climate summits shaping global response, not a specific risk.

items.text: These summits can lead to commitments from numerous countries to meet the objectives of agreements like the Paris Agreement, through adopting country level regulation to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
items.rationale: Generic explanatory statement about outcomes of climate summits, lacks specific business risk.

items.text: In 2025, a putative class action was filed against oil and gas companies, including ConocoPhillips, seeking to hold energy companies liable for increased home insurance premiums allegedly due to climate change losses.
items.rationale: Describes a specific new lawsuit filed against the company alleging climate-related liability, a concrete new legal risk.
